content:
4 hours ago -- The Core Problem: • HTML is just text with tags (markup language) • JavaScript is a programming language (works with objects, functions, variables) • They speak different “languages”! Real-world Analogy: • HTML = Blueprint of a house (static document) • JavaScript = Construction crew (wants to modify the house) • DOM = The actual built house that the crew can walk through and modify Key Point: “The DOM is the bridge that lets JavaScript understand and manipulate HTML.”

Hello

becomes { tagName: “H1”, id: “title”, textContent: “Hello”, style: { color: “”, fontSize: “” }, parentElement: body, children: [], // … many more properties and methods } For after this prefer this https://github.com/chandan0000001/Thunder_Chandan/tree/main/Javascript/Lecture10 in that read all files throughlly an u will understood
